info.title: Platform - Task Management
info.description: The Task Management APIs are used to integrate external systems and streamline the flow of data for tasks created across the OneTrust Platform.

hostname.description: The OneTrust hostname such as app.onetrust.com, app-eu.onetrust.com, app-de.onetrust.com, app-uk.onetrust.com, app-apac.onetrust.com, trial.onetrust.com, or uat.onetrust.com.

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: The Tasks APIs are used to manage tasks, including creation, updates, and retrieval.

post.description:
Use this API to create a new task for an object.

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - This API currently supports issue tasks. Support for tasks on additional objects may be made available in the future.

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

get.description:
Use this API to retrieve a specific task on an object. The response will include details such as the status, priority, and deadline.

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - This API currently supports issue tasks. Support for tasks on additional objects may be made available in the future.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task.

schema.description: UUID format of the task identifier

parameters.name: taskTypeNameKey
parameters.description: The source product of the task.

schema.description: String identifier for the task type service

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

put.description:
Use this API to update an existing task for an object.

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - This API currently supports issue tasks. Support for tasks on additional objects may be made available in the future.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task.

schema.description: UUID format of the task identifier

parameters.name: taskTypeNameKey
parameters.description: The source product of the task.

schema.description: String identifier for the task type service

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

collaboratorId.description: The unique identifier of the user assigned as a collaborator on the task.

collaboratorEmail.description: The email address of the user assigned as a collaborator on the task.

parameters.description: This parameter can be used to store task details in key value pairs.

description.example: Please review the attached document and provide feedback

assigneeId.description: The unique identifier of the user assigned as an assignee on the task.

assigneeEmail.description: The email address of the user assigned as an assignee on the task.

collaborators.description: The details of the collaborators assigned to the task.

deadline.description: The date and time by which the task should be completed.

context.description: The contextual details of the task.

priority.description: The priority level of the task.

isRequired.description: This flag indicates whether the task is required.

sourceType.description: The type of source that created the task.

sourceId.description: The unique identifier of the source that created the task.

example.description: Please review the attached document and provide feedback

valueKey.description: The name key of the attribute used for translation purposes.

date.description: The date and time that the task is due.

color.description: The color code for displaying the task due date.

icon.description: The icon of the task due date.

textKey.description: The text key for the task due date used for translation purposes.

numberOfDays.description: The number of days until or past the task due date.

userInfo.description: The details of the users or user groups responsible for the task.

assigneeId.description: The unique identifier of the user or user group assigned to the task.

type.description: The type of assignee or collaborator.

isCollaborator.description: This flag indicates whether the user or user group was added as a collaborator.

id.description: The unique identifier of the task.

parentEntityId.description: The unique identifier of the parent record where the task was created.

taskType.description: The details of the task type.

deadline.description: The date and time by which the task should be completed.

priority.description: The priority level of the task.

description.example: This task requires reviewing document X and providing feedback

assigneeId.description: The unique identifier of the user assigned as an assignee on the task.

completionDate.description: The date and time that the task was completed.

isRequired.description: This flag indicates whether the task is required.

scopes.ISSUE: Task Management Scope gives the user access to read/write/delete operations used for Task Management.
scopes.ISSUE_READ: Task Management Scope gives the user access to read operations used for Task Management.
